prompts.abstract: A cylindrical object with a rectangular section at the top and a rounded bottom.
prompts.description: A cylindrical object with a rectangular section at the top and a rounded bottom.
prompts.beginner: Design a rectangular section with a rounded bottom. The part measures 0.1875 units in length, 0.75 units in width, and 0.0562 units in height. This component will be used to build a larger structure.
prompts.intermediate: Start by creating a new coordinate system with specific rotations and translations. Draw a two-dimensional sketch that forms a rectangular section with a rounded bottom. The sketch includes an arc and several lines to define the shape. Scale the sketch to fit the desired size and transform it into three dimensions. Extrude the sketch to create a solid body. The final dimensions of the part are a length of 0.1875 units, a width of 0.75 units, and a height of 0.0562 units. This part will serve as a foundational element in the assembly of the final design.
prompts.expert: Create a new coordinate system with Euler angles set to 0.0, 0.0, and -90.0 degrees, and a translation vector of 0.0, 0.0562, and 0.0. Next, draw a two-dimensional sketch on the first face. In the first loop, start with an arc that has a start point at (0.0, 0.0937), a mid point at (0.0663, 0.0663), and an end point at (0.0937, 0.0). Follow this with a line from (0.0937, 0.0) to (0.1875, 0.0), another line from (0.1875, 0.0) to (0.1875, 0.75), a third line from (0.1875, 0.75) to (0.0, 0.75), and a fourth line from (0.0, 0.75) back to (0.0, 0.0937). Scale the entire sketch by a factor of 0.75. Transform the scaled two-dimensional sketch into three dimensions by rotating it using the Euler angles and translating it using the specified translation vector. Finally, extrude the sketch along the normal direction by 0.0562 units to create a solid body. The resulting dimensions are a length of 0.1875 units, a width of 0.75 units, and a height of 0.0562 units.
prompts.all_level_data:
<level1>
Create a new coordinate system with Euler angles set to 0.0, 0.0, and -90.0 degrees, and a translation vector of 0.0, 0.0562, and 0.0. Next, draw a two-dimensional sketch on the first face. In the first loop, start with an arc that has a start point at (0.0, 0.0937), a mid point at (0.0663, 0.0663), and an end point at (0.0937, 0.0). Follow this with a line from (0.0937, 0.0) to (0.1875, 0.0), another line from (0.1875, 0.0) to (0.1875, 0.75), a third line from (0.1875, 0.75) to (0.0, 0.75), and a fourth line from (0.0, 0.75) back to (0.0, 0.0937). Scale the entire sketch by a factor of 0.75. Transform the scaled two-dimensional sketch into three dimensions by rotating it using the Euler angles and translating it using the specified translation vector. Finally, extrude the sketch along the normal direction by 0.0562 units to create a solid body. The resulting dimensions are a length of 0.1875 units, a width of 0.75 units, and a height of 0.0562 units.
</level1>

<level2>
Start by creating a new coordinate system with specific rotations and translations. Draw a two-dimensional sketch that forms a rectangular section with a rounded bottom. The sketch includes an arc and several lines to define the shape. Scale the sketch to fit the desired size and transform it into three dimensions. Extrude the sketch to create a solid body. The final dimensions of the part are a length of 0.1875 units, a width of 0.75 units, and a height of 0.0562 units. This part will serve as a foundational element in the assembly of the final design.
</level2>

<level3>
Design a rectangular section with a rounded bottom. The part measures 0.1875 units in length, 0.75 units in width, and 0.0562 units in height. This component will be used to build a larger structure.
</level3>
prompts.nli_data:
<part_1>
### Construct a Rectangular Section with a Rounded Bottom

#### Step 1: Create a New Coordinate System
- Set the Euler angles to 0.0, 0.0, and -90.0 degrees.
- Set the translation vector to 0.0, 0.0562, and 0.0.

#### Step 2: Draw a 2D Sketch
- **Face 1:**
  - **Loop 1:**
    - **Arc 1:**
      - Start Point: (0.0, 0.0937)
      - Mid Point: (0.0663, 0.0663)
      - End Point: (0.0937, 0.0)
    - **Line 1:**
      - Start Point: (0.0937, 0.0)
      - End Point: (0.1875, 0.0)
    - **Line 2:**
      - Start Point: (0.1875, 0.0)
      - End Point: (0.1875, 0.75)
    - **Line 3:**
      - Start Point: (0.1875, 0.75)
      - End Point: (0.0, 0.75)
    - **Line 4:**
      - Start Point: (0.0, 0.75)
      - End Point: (0.0, 0.0937)

#### Step 3: Scale the 2D Sketch
- Apply a sketch scale of 0.75.

#### Step 4: Transform the Scaled 2D Sketch into 3D
- Rotate the sketch using the Euler angles set in the coordinate system.
- Translate the sketch using the translation vector set in the coordinate system.

#### Step 5: Extrude the 2D Sketch to Generate the 3D Model
- Extrude the sketch towards the normal by 0.0562 units.
- Do not extrude in the opposite direction.
- This operation creates a new solid body.

#### Dimensions
- Length: 0.1875 units
- Width: 0.75 units
- Height: 0.0562 units
</part_1>
